hypothesis: In MADE instantiated on held-out ternary intermetallic systems with a two-fidelity oracle (MLIP cost 1, DFT cost 100), a cost-aware Bayesian optimization agent that models P(Δhull_DFT ≤ 0.01 eV/atom | MLIP-derived features) and allocates 15% of its queries to DFT for on-the-fly surrogate recalibration will discover at least twice as many StructureMatcher-distinct DFT-stable compounds as a MatterGen+diversity MLIP-only baseline under an equal total cost budget of 50 DFT-equivalent evaluations.
